_doc: Model-swap eval matrix. ONE role varied per cell (isolate effects). The JUDGE is held FIXED at baseline across every cell (the measuring instrument) — a cell may NOT override judge_* (the runner rejects it); the judge is pinned via pinned_models below. 'optional' cells run only with --include-optional. RUN is owner-token-gated; the runner only BUILDS the plan unless --execute is passed.
_eval_version_eq_prod_version: GOVERNANCE (owner rule 2026-06-13): this eval validates SPECIFIC dated snapshots. When a pinned model wins, the PROD deploy MUST use that SAME dated snapshot id (not the family alias) — otherwise the eval's Schranken-guarantee does not transfer to what prod runs. Eval version == prod version. Recorded in docs/ops/GOVERNANCE_LOG.md.
_model_ids_verified_2026-06-13: All ids are EXACT provider API strings, web-verified 2026-06-13 (no guessed dates): gpt-5.1-2025-11-13, gpt-4.1-mini-2025-04-14, gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17, gpt-5.4-nano-2026-03-17 (OpenAI dated snapshots); mistral-small-2603 (Mistral Small 4, already a dated string). Cell names use friendly labels; the override VALUE + rate KEY are the exact ids (the meter keys by the model string sent).
_quality_tolerance_note: no-regression tolerance vs baseline (delta >= -tol) applied to BOTH credibility AND answer-quality (the SOFT quality criteria). 0.0 = strict; judge_temperature is 0.0 so judge variance is low. Override at run time with --quality-tolerance. The SCHRANKEN are a HARD safety floor (==1.000) and NEVER get a tolerance.

pinned_models._note: The FROZEN ruler + baseline, applied as base Settings to EVERY cell; per-cell overrides layer on top (a candidate replaces only its varied role). judge_model is pinned HERE (cells cannot touch it) — a drifting ruler invalidates every cross-cell / cross-run comparison. baseline l1/verifier (gpt-5.1) are pinned so the no-regression Δ is measured against a frozen reference.

rates_usd_per_mtok._note: Owner-confirmed published USD-per-1M-token rates ({in, out}), 2026-06-13. Keys are the EXACT dated API model strings the live run sends (the meter keys by model). A model whose rate is absent → est_cost=null + rates_missing[]. NEVER invent prices.
